executive_summary.verdict: DO NOT purchase KenPom subscription at this time
executive_summary.rationale: Barttorvik integration already provides ~90% of KenPom signal at $0 cost. No clear incremental value for betting model.
executive_summary.alternative_recommendation: Continue using Barttorvik via cbbdata.com API. Consider kenpompy as fallback if Barttorvik fails.

pricing.notes: Must register or contact to see pricing. Community suggests $25-95/year range.

endpoints.uncertainty: Full endpoint list only available after purchase

rate_limits.notes: Not mentioned in public documentation. Standard API rate limits likely apply.

repositories.citation: Andrews, Jared. 'kenpompy: A simple yet comprehensive web scraper for kenpom.com.' GitHub, v0.3.5, 2026. https://github.com/j-andrews7/kenpompy
repositories.name: kenpompy
key_features: Web scraper for kenpom.com (not official API)
Summary tables: ratings, team stats, player stats
Miscellaneous stats under Stats and Miscellany headings
Point-in-time historical ratings via archive function

authentication.login_flow: Login function returns authenticated browser session
authentication.limitation: Without subscription, only home page accessible

repositories.architecture: Web scraping with BeautifulSoup and requests
repositories.usage_example:
import kenpompy.utils as kp
browser = kp.login(email, password)
ratings = kp.get_pomeroy_ratings(browser, season='2024')
archive = kp.kp_pomeroy_archive_ratings(date='2024-01-15')
limitations: Web scraping (fragile, could break if HTML changes)

repositories.citation: Weatherman, Andrew. 'cbbdata: CBBData R Package for College Basketball Data.' R Package, 2026. https://cbbdata.aweatherman.com/
repositories.name: cbbdata (R package)

cbd_kenpom_ratings.notes: KenPom authorization no longer required for current ratings

cbd_kenpom_authorization.flow: Pass KenPom password, API confirms active account, logs expiration date

authentication.kenpom_requirement: Email must match KenPom account email to prevent sharing

python_access.workaround: rpy2 bridge to call R functions from Python

limitations: Requires active KenPom subscription for archives

repositories.citation: Sportsdataverse. 'hoopR: Data and Tools for Men's Basketball.' R Package, v2.1.0+, 2026. https://hoopr.sportsdataverse.org/
repositories.name: hoopR

archive_function.name: kp_pomeroy_archive_ratings
archive_function.returns: Data including team rankings, adjusted efficiency metrics, offensive/defensive ratings

repositories.citation: Self, Andrew. 'kenpom-api: KenPom.com API wrapper for Node.js.' npm, 2026. https://www.npmjs.com/package/kenpom-api
repositories.name: kenpom-api (Node.js)

common_patterns: All Python/R libraries use web scraping, not official API
Point-in-time access via archive pages (date parameter)
best_practices: Use official API if available and reasonably priced
kenpompy most mature for Python ecosystem
Always filter by date for point-in-time (avoid look-ahead bias)
Cache results to minimize API/scrape calls
Validate against Barttorvik for correlation check
pitfalls: Web scraping fragile (HTML changes break code)
KenPom ToS may prohibit scraping (check before use)
Cloudflare protection may block automated requests
R packages require rpy2 bridge from Python (complexity)
No free tier — subscription always required
emerging_trends: cbbdata API offering both Barttorvik + KenPom
Custom ridge regression implementations (ESPN box scores)
Ensemble models (Elo + Efficiency) outperforming single metric

AdjEM.name: Adjusted Efficiency Margin
AdjEM.description: Net rating per 100 possessions vs average D-I team

AdjO.name: Adjusted Offensive Efficiency
AdjO.formula: Points per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent quality
AdjO.description: Expected points scored per 100 possessions vs average D-I opponent on neutral court

AdjD.name: Adjusted Defensive Efficiency
AdjD.formula: Points allowed per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent quality
AdjD.description: Expected points allowed per 100 possessions vs average D-I opponent on neutral court

AdjT.name: Adjusted Tempo
AdjT.formula: Possessions per 40 minutes, adjusted for opponent pace
AdjT.description: Expected pace vs team that wants to play at average D-I tempo

Luck.name: Luck Rating
Luck.description: Difference between actual record and Pythagorean expectation

historical_data_availability.implementation: Filter games by date before computing ratings

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Continue using Barttorvik via cbbdata.com API
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Free, Python-native, ~90% KenPom correlation, already integrated, proven ROI +24%

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Barttorvik API fails or 2026 data unavailable
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Most mature Python library, active maintenance, archive access, pandas DataFrames
22-hour investment, 0.85-0.92 correlation, full control, ESPN box scores already available

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need official API for production reliability
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Most stable, no scraping fragility, official support

kenpom_subscription.included: Full website access + data suitable for scraping

cost_benefit_analysis.recommendation: Continue with Barttorvik ($0). KenPom provides <10% incremental signal for $25-150/year. Not cost-effective.

popular_solutions: cbbdata (R package for both Barttorvik + KenPom)
controversial_topics: KenPom vs Barttorvik accuracy debate (~0.95 correlation)
Web scraping ethics and ToS compliance
Custom vs paid metrics for betting models
expert_opinions: Betting models need edge detection, not perfect rankings (0.85 correlation sufficient)
KenPom itself only 60.5% accurate on tight spreads (<7 points)
Barttorvik free alternative captures 90% of signal
Custom implementations offer transparency and control

kenpompy_scraping.risk: Account ban possible if excessive scraping

cbbdata_api.terms: Email matching enforced to prevent account sharing

immediate: NO ACTION REQUIRED — continue with Barttorvik
Monitor Barttorvik API for 2026 data availability
contingency: If Barttorvik fails: Purchase $25 KenPom subscription + kenpompy
If both fail: Implement custom ridge regression (22 hours)
future_research: Contact KenPom for official API pricing (if needed)
Test kenpompy in sandbox environment (if Barttorvik delays)
Validate custom ridge regression correlation vs Barttorvik
